title: WORKTREE_PATH degrades to ProjectRoot (repo root) on the tmux phase path — req.Worktree ≠ the actual cycle worktree; audit every consumer of BridgeRequest.Worktree for silent mis-scoping
summary: Root-cause byproduct of the token-telemetry attribution fix: on the default tmux phase path the transcript's recorded cwd is the real cycle worktree (.evolve/worktrees/cycle-N) but req.Worktree (← WORKTREE_PATH ← env) was NOT that path — subagent/run.go:290 falls back worktreePath=req.ProjectRoot (repo root) when WORKTREE_PATH is empty. The token resolver was the visible victim (exact cwd==Worktree attribution failed → zero input/cache telemetry), now fixed by keying on ArtifactPath instead. But other consumers of BridgeRequest.Worktree / Window.Worktree may be silently mis-scoped by the same degraded value. Investigate: does the orchestrator export WORKTREE_PATH with the provisioned worktree to `evolve subagent run`, or does it rely on cwd only? Fix at the propagation source (export the real worktree) OR make the fallback loud (WARN when it fires) rather than silently substituting ProjectRoot. TDD: a launch whose orchestrator provisions worktree W must yield req.Worktree==W, not ProjectRoot.
acceptance: req.Worktree equals the provisioned cycle worktree on the tmux phase path (not ProjectRoot)
the ProjectRoot fallback either cannot fire for real launches or emits a visible WARN when it does
no other Worktree consumer silently mis-scopes
files: orchestrator dispatch that spawns `evolve subagent run`
connects_to: the ArtifactPath-primary attribution fix (this made the mis-propagation visible)
notes: [REBAND 2026-07-23] 0.86->0.60: priority=low investigation into a degraded-path fallback; not a pipeline-integrity class.
